general_info.definition: Graphene quantum dots (GQDs) are single or few layer graphenes with a tiny size of only several nanometers, possessing properties of both graphene and QDs.

method.conditions: Graphite oxide suspended in DMF(40 ml), ultrasonicated (120 W, 40 kHz) for 30 min, then heated in a Teflon-lined autoclave at 200°C for 24 h.

source: Graphite oxide (GO) powder (prepared by modified Hummers method)

structural.morphology: Single layer graphene sheets (indicated by 0.35 nm height) with uniform size distribution
structural.crystallinity: Graphitic structure with interlayer spacing of 0.21 nm corresponding to the (100) plane.

applications.performance: GQDs encapsulated NiCo2O4 composite achieves 1242 F g⁻¹ at 30 A g⁻¹ and 99% capacitance retention after 4000 cycles.
applications.comparative_advantage: Provides conductive network, improves rate performance and cycling stability compared to pristine NiCo2O4 (which only achieves 790 F g⁻¹ at 30 A g⁻¹).
